capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an access management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether an enterprise access management service exists

questions.prompt: Is the access management service reviewed at least quarterly to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document KPIs and KRIs for the access management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether access management KPIs and KRIs are documented

questions.prompt: Are access management KPIs and KRIs reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether KPIs and KRIs are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Do KPIs or KRIs track orphaned or stale entitlements, access certification completion rate, or mean time to revoke access after a role change or termination signal?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether modern entitlement hygiene and deprovisioning metrics are tracked

questions.prompt: How does the organization measure access certification campaign completion and policy drift across enforcement points?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether certification completion and policy drift are measured

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have technology to manage access control for all policy enforcement points (PEPs)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether access control covers all enforcement points
questions.evaluation_focus: Access control for all enforcement points

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise use a centralized policy decision point (PDP) for access control decisions, or are decision makers siloed by location or system?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether policy decisions are centralized or siloed
questions.evaluation_focus: Centralized vs siloed policy decision points

questions.prompt: Can access control policies be changed dynamically and deployed to enforcement points?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether dynamic access control deployment is supported

questions.prompt: Are access management system (PDP) configurations reviewed at least quarterly to align with authentication, authorization, network, and application access policies?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether PDP configurations are reviewed against governing policies
questions.evaluation_focus: PDP configuration review aligned to policies

questions.prompt: Can the access management system push abstracted policies to enforcement points that translate them for each underlying technology?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether abstracted policy push to PEPs is supported
questions.evaluation_focus: Abstracted policy push to enforcement points

questions.prompt: Are access policies managed as version-controlled policy-as-code (for example via GitOps or infrastructure-as-code pipelines)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether policy-as-code practices are used for access policies

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have documented, formalized need-to-know processes for provisioning network, endpoint, and application access?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether unified need-to-know provisioning processes exist for network and application resources
questions.evaluation_focus: Formalized network and endpoint need-to-know access process

questions.prompt: Is the access request and approval process consistent when requesting network or application access across all locations and systems?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether provisioning is consistent across locations and systems
questions.evaluation_focus: Consistent access provisioning across locations and systems

questions.prompt: Are automated self-service portals available for network and application access requests?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether self-service access provisioning is automated

questions.prompt: Are network and application access provisioning processes reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether provisioning processes are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Does the application access provisioning process cover SaaS and cloud-hosted applications with the same rigor as on-premises systems?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether SaaS and cloud application access is included in formalized provisioning

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise adopt identity-based access control, and do all enforcement points deploy identity-based rules?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether identity-based access control is adopted enterprise-wide at all PEPs
questions.evaluation_focus: Identity-based rules at all enforcement points

questions.prompt: Is device posture (health, compliance, or trust level) evaluated as part of network and remote access decisions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether device posture is incorporated into access decisions
questions.evaluation_focus: Device posture in network and remote access

questions.prompt: Is subject posture considered consistently when accessing the network from all locations?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether posture checks apply from all access locations
questions.evaluation_focus: Subject posture considered from all locations

questions.prompt: Are enterprise asset attributes (classification, sensitivity, ownership) used in application access decisions for all subjects?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether asset attributes inform application access for all subjects
questions.evaluation_focus: Asset attributes considered for all subjects

capability_questions.capability_name: Remote access and zero-trust network access

questions.prompt: Is there a documented, formalized access process for remote users?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal remote access process exists

questions.prompt: Do remote users receive the same access policy across all gateways (cloud, data center, on-premises) and when accessing from a trusted LAN segment?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether remote access policy is consistent across gateways and LAN
questions.evaluation_focus: Consistent remote access policy across gateways and LAN

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise use cloud secure service edge (SSE) or zero-trust network access (ZTNA) to provide access to internal trusted assets?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether SSE or ZTNA is used for internal asset access
questions.evaluation_focus: Cloud SSE or ZTNA for internal asset access

questions.prompt: Does remote access use zero-trust network access (ZTNA/SSE) with identity and device posture checks, rather than legacy perimeter VPN as the primary control?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether zero-trust remote access replaces legacy VPN as the primary control
questions.evaluation_focus: Zero-trust remote access with identity and posture checks

capability_questions.capability_name: Access Isolation (Separation of duties) and its automation

questions.prompt: Is there a clear process separating user access between production, development, and deployment environments?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether production, development, and deployment access is separated
questions.evaluation_focus: User separation across production, development, and deployment

questions.prompt: Are service accounts and normal user accounts given separate permissions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether service and normal accounts have separate permissions
questions.evaluation_focus: Separate permissions for service and normal accounts

questions.prompt: Does the CI/CD pipeline automatically remove development users from production access when code is promoted to production?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether CI/CD automates removal of dev access from production
questions.evaluation_focus: Automated CI/CD removal of dev access from production

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise maintain a granular subject-to-object access control matrix?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a granular access matrix exists

questions.prompt: Does the access matrix apply enterprise-wide across all locations and functions, not just specific segments?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the access matrix scope is enterprise-wide

questions.prompt: Are access control matrices reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether access matrices are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Does the organization run periodic access recertification campaigns where data or resource owners approve or revoke entitlements?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether periodic access recertification campaigns are conducted

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized, documented external or guest user onboarding process?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal guest onboarding process exists

questions.prompt: Are guest user access timeframes and access control provisioning and deprovisioning automated?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether guest access lifecycle is automated

questions.prompt: Is there a documented, formalized process to grant subjects access to the out-of-band (OOB) management network?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal OOB access process exists

questions.prompt: Are secure protocols and jump hosts or privileged access controls used for OOB access?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether secure protocols and privileged access controls protect OOB access

questions.prompt: Do subjects receive the same OOB access control regardless of source location (LAN, VPN, cloud)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether OOB access control is consistent across source locations
questions.evaluation_focus: Consistent OOB access control across source locations

questions.prompt: Are OOB access controls reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether OOB access controls are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Is there a documented, formalized process to grant subjects access to data?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal data access process exists

questions.prompt: Is data access consistent for a subject regardless of location?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether data access is location-independent

questions.prompt: Is the data access process and policy reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether data access processes are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a dedicated access management service manager, separate from authentication, credential management, and directory or identity provider administration, to maintain separation of duties?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a dedicated access management custodian exists with separation of duties

capability_questions.capability_name: Access, authorization and accounting and logging

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging access activity and lifecycle changes to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether access logging to the SOC is formally documented

questions.prompt: Are access logs and authorization activity transmitted securely to the SOC? (This is distinct from authentication or credential creation logs.)
questions.intent: Evaluate whether access activity is logged securely to the SOC
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure access activity logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Are dynamic access policy changes captured and transferred to the SOC in near-real time?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether dynamic access changes are captured and sent to the SOC

questions.prompt: Are anomalous access policy changes or bulk entitlement grants detected and alerted in near-real time (including AI-assisted analytics)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether anomalous access changes trigger near-real-time detection and alerting

questions.prompt: Are workload and service principal access permissions tied to a documented lifecycle (creation, rotation, and deprovisioning)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether non-human workload identities have lifecycle-bound access
questions.evaluation_focus: Workload and service principal access lifecycle

questions.prompt: How does the organization govern API keys, OAuth client credentials, and other non-interactive access tokens?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether API keys and OAuth credentials are governed
questions.evaluation_focus: API key and OAuth client credential governance

questions.prompt: Are automation accounts and AI agent identities subject to explicit access boundaries and approval before accessing enterprise resources?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether automation and AI agent accounts have defined access boundaries
questions.evaluation_focus: Automation and AI agent account access boundaries

questions.prompt: Does the organization distinguish approved AI tools from shadow AI tools that may access enterprise data, and are access controls enforced accordingly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether approved versus shadow AI tool access is governed
questions.evaluation_focus: Approved versus shadow AI tool access to enterprise data

questions.prompt: Is there a policy governing delegated agent actions on behalf of users, including what resources agents may access and under what approval conditions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether delegated agent access actions are governed by policy
questions.evaluation_focus: Policy for delegated agent actions on behalf of users
